title: Golden Globes 2026: One Battle After Another Takes Home Four Awards
text: "One Battle After Another," Paul Thomas Anderson's satirical film about radical politics, emerged as a major winner at the 83rd Golden Globe Awards, securing four awards across multiple categories. The Warner Bros. Discovery production claimed the top prize for Best Picture (Musical or Comedy). Anderson himself received dual recognition, winning both Best Director and Best Screenplay for the film. Teyana Taylor earned Best Supporting Actress for her role as a revolutionary character in the film. In her acceptance speech, Taylor delivered an emotional message: "To my Brown sisters and little brown girls watching tonight, our softness is not a liability." "Hamnet," another major contender, won Best Picture (Drama), with Jessie Buckley taking home Best Actress for her portrayal of a grieving mother. The film secured two total awards. In television, "The Pitt" (HBO Max) won Best TV Series (Drama), while "The Studio" (Apple TV) took Best TV Series (Musical or Comedy). Netflix's "Adolescence" dominated the limited series category with four awards, including a historic win for 16-year-old Owen Cooper as the youngest ever recipient in the Best Supporting Actor in a TV Drama category.
